ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002764 matches 'chicken nugget' as the core food item; the meal context (10-piece set) is not separately coded in FoodOn.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for chicken nuggets meal; SNOMED CT does not maintain a granular fast-food product hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 170 g, representing a 10-piece chicken nuggets meal as typically served at a fast-food outlet in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: 10Pc Chicken Nuggets Meal (D) Original — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for '10Pc Chicken Nuggets Meal (D) Original' (cr_id G00010015). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for chicken nuggets (FDC ID 171477) where HPB does not report these fields.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific 10-piece chicken nuggets meal product. GI of 66 is estimated using the closest analogous entry for breaded, deep-fried chicken nuggets from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008; Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The breaded wheat-flour coating is the primary glycaemic driver. GL per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 66 × (14.3 × 1.70) / 100 = 66 × 9.7 / 100 ≈ 6.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this product; a single 170 g serving delivers approximately 1,204 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore HPB, and dipping sauces will further increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High fat content (22.6 g/100 g), refined wheat-flour breading with medium GI (66), and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) impair insulin sensitivity and raise postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of this product; the deep-fried breaded coating raises postprandial blood glucose and the high saturated and trans fat content worsens insulin resistance — grilled or baked chicken without breading is a clinically preferable alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined burden of high sodium (708 mg/100 g), saturated fat (~5.2 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g), and cholesterol (50 mg/100 g) is unfavourable for cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this product; the combination of trans fat, saturated fat, high sodium, and cholesterol contributes to LDL elevation, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated blood pressure, all of which are independent cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and the trans fat content is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried breaded chicken products; AGEs generated during deep-frying and the estimated trans fat content (0.3 g/100 g) promote systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 10-Piece Chicken Nuggets Meal (Original) is a fast-food meal sold in Singapore consisting of ten deep-fried, breaded chicken nuggets served as a set meal. The nuggets are made from processed chicken meat coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried in vegetable oil, yielding a crispy golden exterior with a moist, tender chicken interior. The flavour profile is savoury and mildly salty with a characteristic fried-food richness. Per 100 g, the product provides 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g fat, and 14.3 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 708 mg. The product is classified as ultra-processed and is halal-certified. It is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and refined-carbohydrate content. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), driven by the wheat-flour breading. This product is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Singapore and is widely available at fast-food chains. It is not vegetarian or vegan, contains gluten, and is dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 10 chicken nuggets?
Is chicken nuggets meal suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010015) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy closest analogue (chicken nuggets, FDC ID 171477) and are clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product, and no exact match available in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo source record for this product and no approved secondary source provides exact values for this specific fast-food meal formulation. trans_fat_g of 0.3 g/100 g is an estimate based on typical deep-fried breaded chicken nugget formulations as reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477); this value is not explicitly stated in the HPB source. saturated_fat_g of 5.2 g/100 g and dietary_fibre_g of 0.6 g/100 g are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) as the HPB record does not report these fields. Serving size of 170 g is estimated based on a standard 10-piece chicken nuggets portion weight; HPB does not specify a per-serving weight for this entry. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026.

improvement_actions_taken: Added canonical English food_name normalising from menu-style input.
Added all mandatory Singaporean local-language names: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Populated aliases and aliases_by_language with full multilingual coverage.

Added GI (66, medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008.
Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving (6.4) based on 170 g serving size.
Renamed health condition from 'diabetes' to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes'.
